response.justification: The candidate is a senior engineer with roughly 15+ years of experience applying to an entry-level position, which raises serious concerns about role fit, compensation expectations, and retention. More critically, the role requires strong verbal and written German while the resume lists German as only basic, and his professional depth lies in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang rather than Java, which appears only as a listed skill with minimal demonstrated project use. He would likely excel technically, but he does not match this junior, German-speaking, Java-focused consulting profile.
strengths: Completed computer science degree plus deep, broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, and embedded systems
Strong agile and software-craftsmanship background (TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Fluent English with proven client-facing, teaching, and workshop experience
concerns: German is listed as basic, while the role requires strong verbal and written German
Heavily overqualified for an entry-level role, creating flight-risk and salary-mismatch concerns
Limited demonstrated professional Java work; core stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang

key_factors.factor: Strong OOP and agile engineering fundamentals
